name: System Reminder: Team Shutdown
description: System reminder for team shutdown
audit_details: Instructs the agent running in non-interactive mode that it cannot return its final response to the user until its subagent team members are completely shut down. It outlines steps to request shutdown gracefully, wait for approvals, and run a cleanup operation.
gemini_rationale: Swarm coordination and teammate lifecycle management are also present in the Google Antigravity SDK. However, teammate termination is managed programmatically via the manage_subagents tool or the subagent communication pipeline rather than requestShutdown.
gemini_strategy: We adapt teammate shutdown in the Antigravity SDK. The coordinator is instructed to call manage_subagents with Action='kill_all' or wait for subagents to report completion, cleaning up local git worktrees before returning the final report.

name: System Reminder: Thinking frequency tuning
description: Instructs Claude to treat system-reminder tags as harness instructions and calibrate thinking frequency based on task complexity
audit_details: Instructs the model on how to handle harness-appended system-reminder tags requesting it to bypass the thinking block on simple tasks. It teaches the model to treat these as internal settings and dynamically tune thinking budgets.
gemini_rationale: Gemini 2.0/3.5 models support configurable thinking budgets via API parameters. If a task is trivial, disabling extended thinking reduces latency and token consumption, which can be configured directly rather than using system-reminder tags.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by dynamically adjusting the thinking_config (e.g. setting thinking_budget to 0 for simple tasks or non-reasoning models) in the LocalAgentConfig based on the query complexity, mapping Claude's tag-based instructions to native Gemini parameters.

name: System Reminder: Token usage
description: Current token usage statistics
audit_details: Displays the current number of used, total, and remaining tokens to keep the agent aware of its context limits and pacing.
gemini_rationale: Tracking token limits is crucial for preventing context truncation. In the Antigravity SDK, token counts are tracked natively in the Conversation object metadata rather than formatting them into raw string system reminders.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by retrieving usage statistics from the Gemini API response metadata after each turn and logging it to the console, alerting the model via system messages if the context window is near 85% capacity.

name: System Reminder: Ultraplan mode
description: System reminder for using Ultraplan mode to create a detailed implementation plan with multi-agent exploration and critique.
audit_details: Instructs the agent to enter Ultraplan mode, using multiple parallel subagents to explore codebase aspects, locate modified files, and review code design changes simultaneously for highly thorough planning.
gemini_rationale: Instead of spawning heavy Docker subagents for parallel exploration (which adds latency), Gemini can perform high-throughput searches and directory mapping in parallel using parallel tool calls within a single context.
gemini_strategy: We adapt Ultraplan mode into the Antigravity planning framework. The coordinator invokes multiple parallel grep_search and list_dir tools in a single turn, aggregating findings to build the implementation plan without separate agent containers.

name: System Reminder: USD budget
description: Current USD budget statistics
audit_details: Shows the current spend budget in USD, detailing used, total, and remaining funds to prevent runaway API billing during background agent runs.
gemini_rationale: Cost control is essential for background cron agents. The Antigravity SDK has built-in cost tracking hooks that measure API usage and enforce budget constraints at the client level.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by injecting cost warnings into the agent's pre-turn instructions when usage exceeds 75% of the allocated budget, preventing the model from starting new complex loops if the remaining budget is too low.

name: Tool Description: Agent (simple usage notes)
description: Simplified usage notes for the Agent tool, including when to delegate, fork behavior, resumption, worktree isolation, background execution, parallel launches, and context restrictions
audit_details: Provides simplified usage notes for the Agent delegation tool, outlining when to delegate complex multi-file tasks vs. searching directly, and summarizing fork behavior, worktree isolation, and background run limitations.
gemini_rationale: Subagent spawning is supported natively in the Antigravity SDK via invoke_subagent. We need to adapt the delegation heuristics to prevent unnecessary subagent overhead for simple file checks.
gemini_strategy: We adapt these usage notes for the invoke_subagent tool definition. We instruct the agent to use direct search tools for local file lookups and reserve invoke_subagent for multi-step tasks requiring isolated branches or separate contexts.

name: Tool Description: Agent (usage notes)
description: Usage notes and instructions for the Task/Agent tool, including guidance on launching subagents, background execution, resumption, and worktree isolation
audit_details: Detailed usage instructions for the Agent tool, covering subagent naming, result reporting, worktree mapping, and asynchronous background execution limits.
gemini_rationale: The Antigravity SDK uses the invoke_subagent capability to launch background agents with custom workspaces (inherit, branch, share). The guidelines for running tasks asynchronously must align with the SDK tool interface.
gemini_strategy: We map these instructions to the invoke_subagent parameter guidelines. The agent is directed to use branch workspace for isolated coding tasks and share workspace for joint development, tracking results via the send_message tool.

name: Tool Description: AskUserQuestion (preview field)
description: Instructions for using the HTML preview field on single-select question options to display visual artifacts like UI mockups, code snippets, and diagrams
audit_details: Instructs on using the preview field on AskUserQuestion options to render self-contained HTML fragments (such as UI mockups or code diffs) for side-by-side user comparison.
gemini_rationale: HTML preview rendering is supported by the Antigravity CLI and chat UI during interactive questions, making this a highly valuable feature for design alignment.
gemini_strategy: Adopt the preview guidelines. Instruct the agent to format code options and mockups inside the preview HTML string when using ask_question, ensuring no disallowed script tags are present.

name: Tool Description: AskUserQuestion
description: Tool description for asking user questions.
audit_details: Explains how and when to use the AskUserQuestion tool to clarify requirements, gather preferences, or present design choices to the user.
gemini_rationale: Interactive questioning is mapped directly to the ask_question tool in the Antigravity SDK, prompting the user with a modal select menu.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by using the SDK's native ask_question tool, maintaining guidelines to structure options as clear choices and avoid trivial yes/no text questions.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — communication)
description: Bash tool alternative: output text directly instead of echo/printf
audit_details: Advises the model to output text communication directly in its response block rather than running echo or printf shell commands.
gemini_rationale: Running terminal commands for simple communication adds unnecessary overhead and safety risk. Direct response generation is native to LLMs.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this as a safety policy constraint. The pre_tool_call hook intercepts echo/printf commands used for talking to the user and reminds the model to output text directly.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — content search)
description: Bash tool alternative: use Grep for content search instead of grep/rg
audit_details: Advises the model to use the dedicated Grep tool instead of running raw grep or rg commands in the bash terminal.
gemini_rationale: The Antigravity SDK exposes grep_search as a structured tool, which is safer, faster, and more reliable than raw shell searches.
gemini_strategy: We adopt this restriction by enforcing the grep_search tool in our planning and safety skills, and configuring the shell safety monitor to block raw grep/rg execution.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — edit files)
description: Bash tool alternative: use Edit for file editing instead of sed/awk
audit_details: Instructs the model to use the dedicated Edit tool instead of running raw sed, awk, or perl commands in the bash shell.
gemini_rationale: Direct shell stream editing is prone to syntax errors and path escapes. The replace_file_content and multi_replace_file_content tools provide safe, structured modifications.
gemini_strategy: We enforce the use of replace_file_content and multi_replace_file_content for all editing tasks, while restricting sed/awk in the shell security policy.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — file search)
description: Bash tool alternative: use Glob for file search instead of find/ls
audit_details: Directs the agent to use the Glob tool instead of running raw find or ls commands in the shell to search for files.
gemini_rationale: Structured file listing is handled by list_dir in the Antigravity SDK, preventing parsing errors and command injection risks.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this rule by mapping file search requests to list_dir and grep_search, blocking shell find/ls in the safety policies.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — read files)
description: Bash tool alternative: use Read for file reading instead of cat/head/tail
audit_details: Directs the agent to use the dedicated Read tool instead of running cat, head, or tail shell commands to view file contents.
gemini_rationale: Viewing files is handled by view_file in the Antigravity SDK, which manages token limits and formats output cleanly.
gemini_strategy: We map all file reading tasks to view_file, ensuring that shell commands like cat/head are blocked by the pre-tool safety hook.

name: Tool Description: Bash (alternative — write files)
description: Bash tool alternative: use Write for file writing instead of echo/cat
audit_details: Directs the agent to use the dedicated Write tool instead of running echo > file or cat << EOF in the bash shell to create files.
gemini_rationale: Writing new files is handled by the write_to_file tool in the Antigravity SDK, providing structured file creation and permission tracking.
gemini_strategy: We enforce the use of write_to_file for all file creation tasks, blocking shell redirection operators in the shell parser.

name: Tool Description: Bash (built-in tools note)
description: Note that built-in tools provide better UX than Bash equivalents
audit_details: Reinforces the guideline that built-in tools (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep) should be preferred over bash equivalents because they provide better tracking and safety.
gemini_rationale: Built-in tools run inside the SDK runtime, giving the user visibility and granular permission controls that raw shell commands lack.
gemini_strategy: We embed this note in the tool guidelines. If the agent attempts a shell command for file operations, the pre-execution loop rejects it and directs the agent to the corresponding built-in tool.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — adjust settings)
description: Work with user to adjust sandbox settings on failure
audit_details: Instructs the agent to work with the user to adjust sandbox settings when a shell command fails due to sandbox security restrictions.
gemini_rationale: Sandbox adjustment aligns with the ask_permission flow in the Antigravity SDK, which allows the agent to request elevated permissions.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by directing the agent to invoke ask_permission when a command fails with a permission error, requesting the narrowest possible target scope.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — evidence: access denied)
description: Sandbox evidence: access denied to paths outside allowed directories
audit_details: Lists access denied to paths outside allowed directories as evidence of sandbox restrictions causing a command failure.
gemini_rationale: Detecting path permission failures is key to triggering elevation prompts. In the Antigravity SDK, path check errors are captured by the tool runtime.
gemini_strategy: We implement string matching in the python tool runner to detect permission errors and map them to standard system suggestions for the agent.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — evidence list header)
description: Header for list of sandbox-caused failure evidence
audit_details: Provides a header for listing the evidence that a command's failure was caused by sandbox restrictions.
gemini_rationale: Consistent error header parsing helps the model distinguish code errors from security restrictions.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by standardizing sandbox restriction messages returned to the agent under a clear system warning header.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — evidence: network failures)
description: Sandbox evidence: network connection failures to non-whitelisted hosts
audit_details: Lists network connection failures to non-whitelisted hosts as evidence of sandbox restrictions.
gemini_rationale: If a curl command fails due to network blocking, the agent must understand it is a safety constraint and not a bug in the code.
gemini_strategy: We detect network socket errors in the run_command wrapper and return a clean safety error message, instructing the agent to suggest alternative offline tools.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — evidence: operation not permitted)
description: Sandbox evidence: operation not permitted errors
audit_details: Lists 'Operation not permitted' errors as evidence of sandbox restrictions.
gemini_rationale: Standardizing operation failures helps the agent recognize sandbox limits.
gemini_strategy: We map EPERM and operation failures in run_command to a security warning in the tool output, prompting the agent to degrade gracefully.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — evidence: unix socket errors)
description: Sandbox evidence: unix socket connection errors
audit_details: Lists Unix socket connection errors as evidence of sandbox restrictions.
gemini_rationale: Unix socket blockages prevent access to local daemons. The agent must recognize this as a security boundary.
gemini_strategy: We capture socket errors in run_command and present them as a sandbox block, instructing the agent to use local file or command tools.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — failure evidence condition)
description: Condition: command failed with evidence of sandbox restrictions
audit_details: Defines the condition where a command failed specifically due to sandbox restrictions, separating sandbox issues from code bugs.
gemini_rationale: Distinguishing compile bugs from security blockages is critical to prevent the model from wasting time editing code when a permission change is needed.
gemini_strategy: We handle this at the tool wrapper level by returning a specific system error block for sandbox failures, prompting the model to use the ask_permission workflow.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — mandatory mode)
description: Policy: all commands must run in sandbox mode
audit_details: Declares that all commands must run in sandbox mode and that disabling the sandbox is blocked by global safety policy.
gemini_rationale: The Antigravity SDK enforces command sandboxing by default. The run_command tool requires user approval and cannot run unsandboxed without explicit configuration.
gemini_strategy: We enforce this rule at the SDK level. Unsandboxed command execution triggers the unsandboxed permission request, and the agent is instructed to respect this gate.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — no exceptions)
description: Commands cannot run outside sandbox under any circumstances
audit_details: Declares that commands cannot run outside the sandbox under any circumstances.
gemini_rationale: Strict safety boundaries protect the host machine from malicious command executions.
gemini_strategy: We enforce sandboxing at the tool configuration level. The run_command tool executes in a restricted environment, and no parameter bypasses are exposed to the prompt.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — no sensitive paths)
description: Do not suggest adding sensitive paths to sandbox allowlist
audit_details: Warns the agent not to suggest adding sensitive paths (such as SSH keys, shell profiles, or credential files) to the sandbox allowlist.
gemini_rationale: Allowing sensitive paths exposes credentials to prompt injection attacks.
gemini_strategy: We implement hard path blocks in the write_file and read_file permission checkers, rejecting any request targeting ~/.ssh, ~/.bashrc, or similar sensitive folders.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — per-command)
description: Treat each command individually; default to sandbox for future commands
audit_details: Instructs the agent to treat each unsandboxed execution individually and to default to the sandbox for future commands.
gemini_rationale: Restricting elevation persistence prevents privilege escalation across turns.
gemini_strategy: We enforce this in the SDK permission manager. Unsandboxed permission is transient and expires after the turn, requiring the agent to request it again if needed.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — response header)
description: Header for how to respond when seeing sandbox-caused failures
audit_details: Sets the header instruction for how the agent should respond when a sandbox-caused failure occurs.
gemini_rationale: Aligns agent reasoning when encountering sandbox rejections.
gemini_strategy: We prepend sandbox response instructions to the tool feedback when a command is blocked, guiding the agent to request narrow scope permission.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — retry without sandbox)
description: Immediately retry with dangerouslyDisableSandbox on sandbox failure
audit_details: Instructs the agent to immediately retry the command without the sandbox if it fails due to sandbox constraints.
gemini_rationale: If a command fails and requires elevation, retrying immediately using run_command exposes the elevation request to the user's terminal approval loop.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by directing the agent to run the command directly via run_command, which automatically triggers the user terminal approval step in the Antigravity SDK.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — tmpdir)
description: Use $TMPDIR for temporary files in sandbox mode
audit_details: Instructs the agent to always use the $TMPDIR environment variable instead of /tmp for writing temporary files in sandbox mode.
gemini_rationale: Writing to /tmp directly is blocked by default sandbox directories. Using the designated $TMPDIR ensures that files are written to safe, writable locations.
gemini_strategy: We map this to the Antigravity workspace structure. The agent is directed to use the workspace scratch folder or the local $TMPDIR path for all temporary scripts.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sandbox — user permission prompt)
description: Note that disabling sandbox will prompt user for permission
audit_details: Reminds the agent that disabling the sandbox will trigger a user permission prompt.
gemini_rationale: Keeping the agent aware of user gates helps it design non-interactive scripts.
gemini_strategy: We document this boundary in the run_command tool details. The model is warned that commands run asynchronously or require terminal approval before execution.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sleep — keep short)
description: Bash tool instruction: keep sleep duration to 1-5 seconds
audit_details: Advises keeping sleep duration short (1-5 seconds) to avoid blocking the execution loop.
gemini_rationale: Passive sleeping blocks execution threads and wastes API call steps. Keeping sleeps short reduces latency.
gemini_strategy: We enforce this at the hook level. Shell command executions containing sleep over 5 seconds are blocked, prompting the model to use the schedule tool instead.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sleep — no polling background tasks)
description: Bash tool instruction: do not poll background tasks, wait for notification
audit_details: Instructs the agent not to poll background tasks by sleeping, as the harness will notify it automatically upon completion.
gemini_rationale: The Antigravity SDK notifies the model when background tasks finish, making polling loops redundant and expensive.
gemini_strategy: We adopt this by instructing the agent to yield execution when launching async background commands, letting the SDK resume the agent when the task output arrives.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sleep — run immediately)
description: Bash tool instruction: do not sleep between commands that can run immediately
audit_details: Bans sleeping between commands that can be run immediately, encouraging immediate execution.
gemini_rationale: Saves execution time and avoids unnecessary turns.
gemini_strategy: We enforce immediate execution in our command guidelines, prompting the model to chain commands or execute them sequentially in a single turn.

name: Tool Description: Bash (sleep — use check commands)
description: Bash tool instruction: use check commands rather than sleeping when polling
audit_details: Advises using check commands (such as viewing status or logs) rather than sleeping when polling a process.
gemini_rationale: Direct inspection provides real-time state updates, which is much faster than guessing durations with sleep.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by directing the agent to use CLI check commands (e.g. git status, task status queries) instead of sleeping.

name: Tool Description: BrowserBatch
description: Tool description for BrowserBatch, which executes multiple browser tool calls sequentially in one round trip
audit_details: Describes the BrowserBatch tool for executing multiple browser tool calls sequentially in one round trip to save turn latency.
gemini_rationale: Fanning out multiple browser operations (navigation, click, type, screenshot) in one step is supported by the Chrome DevTools MCP tools.
gemini_strategy: We map BrowserBatch to the Chrome DevTools MCP. The agent can chain browser actions in a single Python script or tool block, capturing screenshots to verify outcomes.

name: Tool Description: Computer
description: Main description for the Chrome browser computer automation tool
audit_details: Main description for the computer use browser tool, detailing coordinate selection from screenshots and handling page load failures.
gemini_rationale: Browser automation is supported by the chrome-devtools-plugin in our workspace, providing coordinate-based clicks and text typing.
gemini_strategy: We adapt these computer instructions to the Chrome DevTools MCP toolset. The agent uses screenshots to locate elements and navigates page transitions using wait commands.

name: Tool Description: EnterPlanMode
description: Tool description for entering plan mode to explore and design implementation approaches
audit_details: Describes the EnterPlanMode tool, which transitions the session into Plan Mode for exploring the codebase and compiling a design plan for user approval.
gemini_rationale: Planning mode gates the development process to prevent incorrect code modifications. The Antigravity SDK implements plan mode using dedicated task checkpoints.
gemini_strategy: We map EnterPlanMode to our custom planning hooks. The agent is directed to enter plan mode for non-trivial modifications, writing the plan to implementation_plan.md.

name: Tool Description: EnterWorktree
description: Tool description for the EnterWorktree tool.
audit_details: Describes the EnterWorktree tool for creating an isolated git worktree and switching the current session into it when instructed by the user or project rules.
gemini_rationale: Worktree isolation is key for managing concurrent tasks or branch development. The Antigravity SDK supports this in invoke_subagent using 'branch' or 'share' workspaces.
gemini_strategy: We adapt worktree commands to the invoke_subagent Workspace parameter. The agent uses Workspace='branch' to create isolated environments for subagent coding tasks.

name: Tool Description: ExitPlanMode
description: Description for the ExitPlanMode tool, which presents a plan dialog for the user to approve
audit_details: Describes the ExitPlanMode tool, which signals plan completion and submits the implementation plan for user approval.
gemini_rationale: Exiting planning mode is the gate to starting the execution phase. The Antigravity SDK handles this via the planning skill verification steps.
gemini_strategy: We map ExitPlanMode to our planning skill wrapper. The agent calls ExitPlanMode to submit the written plan, prompting the user for approval before continuing.

name: Tool Description: ExitWorktree
description: Roughly, the reverse of the ExitWorktree
audit_details: Describes the ExitWorktree tool, which closes the isolated worktree session and returns the agent to the original working directory.
gemini_rationale: Closing workspaces cleans up git references and restores the agent's context path.
gemini_strategy: We map ExitWorktree to the completion of subagent runs. The parent agent handles the cleanup automatically when the subagent task exits.

name: Tool Description: NotebookEdit
description: Tool description for editing Jupyter notebook cells
audit_details: Describes the NotebookEdit tool for editing individual cells in Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb files) using insert or replace modes.
gemini_rationale: Jupyter cell-level editing is not natively supported in the core Antigravity SDK, which focuses on standard Python script editing. It is bypassed to avoid tool bloat.
gemini_strategy: Bypass this tool. For Jupyter notebook modifications, the agent is directed to use standard python libraries to parse and write notebook JSON, or write script-based edits.

name: Tool Description: PowerShell
description: Describes the PowerShell command execution tool with syntax guidance, timeout settings, and instructions to prefer specialized tools over PowerShell for file operations
audit_details: Describes the PowerShell command execution tool, providing syntax guidance, timeout parameters, and warnings to avoid using it for file operations.
gemini_rationale: PowerShell commands are needed on Windows environments. The Antigravity SDK wraps terminal execution dynamically based on the host OS.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by registering Windows command wrappers in the SDK, maintaining the same warnings to prefer built-in file tools over shell cmdlets.

name: Tool Description: RemoteTrigger prompt
description: Tool prompt for calling the claude.ai RemoteTrigger API to list, get, create, update, or run scheduled remote agent routines
audit_details: Describes the RemoteTrigger tool for managing scheduled agent routines on Claude.ai remote sandboxed infrastructure.
gemini_rationale: The Claude.ai remote triggers are proprietary to Anthropic's cloud. In the Google Antigravity SDK, background scheduling is handled locally or in GCP worktrees.
gemini_strategy: Bypass the RemoteTrigger API. We use the local Antigravity schedule tool to run background tasks and manage timers.

name: Tool Description: request_teach_access (part of teach mode)
description: Describes a tool that requests permission to guide the user through a task step-by-step using fullscreen tooltip overlays instead of direct access
audit_details: Describes request_teach_access for guiding the user through tasks using fullscreen tooltip overlays instead of direct command execution.
gemini_rationale: Teach overlays are specific to the Claude Code desktop UI and have no equivalent in the Antigravity CLI or SDK.
gemini_strategy: Bypass this tool. The agent uses standard text responses or step-by-step instructions in the chat view to guide users who want to learn.

name: Tool Description: SendMessageTool (non-agent-teams)
description: Send a message the user will read, describes this tool well.
audit_details: Describes the user-facing SendMessageTool for sending markdown messages and file attachments to the user's chat window.
gemini_rationale: Sending messages to the user is the primary communication mechanism. In the Antigravity SDK, this maps directly to response text and artifact creation.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by using the standard agent chat output loop, formatting rich markdown responses and referencing absolute paths of generated artifact files.

name: Tool Description: SendMessageTool
description: Agent teams version of SendMessageTool.
audit_details: Describes the agent-to-agent SendMessageTool for communicating with other subagent team members in a swarm.
gemini_rationale: Agent-to-agent messaging is supported in the Antigravity SDK via the send_message tool to communicate between running subagents.
gemini_strategy: We map this directly to the SDK's send_message tool, ensuring that subagents can coordinate asynchronously using their conversation IDs.

name: Tool Description: SendUserFile
description: Describes the SendUserFile tool for surfacing generated deliverable files to the user, with optional captions and normal or proactive status
audit_details: Describes the SendUserFile tool for surfacing generated deliverables (diagrams, reports, screenshots) to the user's interface with captions.
gemini_rationale: Surfacing files as visual artifacts is supported by the Antigravity SDK's artifact directory mapping.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by writing deliverables directly to the designated artifact directory and providing markdown file links in the final message.

name: Tool Description: Skill
description: Tool description for executing skills in the main conversation
audit_details: Describes the skill tool used to invoke slash commands and custom skills in the conversation.
gemini_rationale: The Antigravity SDK registers custom skills as Python functions or subagents, which are called natively during tool execution.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by registering custom skills directly in the LocalAgentConfig's tools, allowing the agent to invoke them like any other tool.

name: Tool Description: TaskList (teammate workflow)
description: Conditional section appended to TaskList tool description
audit_details: Outlines the workflow for teammates using the TaskList tool to view, claim, and update pending tasks in a shared task queue.
gemini_rationale: Task coordination is handled via project task lists (task.md) or structured task states in the Antigravity SDK.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by directing subagents to read and update a central task.md file in the shared workspace to claim work units.

name: Tool Description: TeamDelete
description: Tool description for the TeamDelete tool
audit_details: Describes the TeamDelete tool for removing team and task directories from the local disk when swarm work is complete.
gemini_rationale: Cleaning up local workspace directories prevents disk clutter. In the Antigravity SDK, this maps to subagent termination and workspace pruning.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by using manage_subagents to terminate subagents, which automatically triggers workspace cleanup at the runner layer.

name: Tool Description: TeammateTool
description: Tool for managing teams and coordinating teammates in a swarm
audit_details: Describes the TeammateTool (TeamCreate) for spawning a swarm of agents to handle complex, parallelizable codebase tasks.
gemini_rationale: Orchestrating agent swarms is supported in the Antigravity SDK by defining and invoking subagents.
gemini_strategy: We map the teammate tool to the SDK's invoke_subagent and define_subagent interfaces, using standard Python control loops to manage the swarm.

name: Tool Description: ToolSearch (second part)
description: The bulk of the tool description.
audit_details: Describes the ToolSearch tool, which matches a query against deferred tools and returns their JSONSchema definitions to make them callable.
gemini_rationale: Deferred tool fetching allows the agent to discover tools dynamically. In the Antigravity SDK, this maps to MCP server tool discovery.
gemini_strategy: We adapt this by querying MCP servers dynamically via the SDK connection layer, fetching and registering schemas on-demand.

name: Tool Description: WebSearch
description: Tool description for web search functionality
audit_details: Describes the WebSearch tool for querying the web to get real-time information and formatting the results as markdown hyperlinks.
gemini_rationale: Web searching is a core information retrieval capability. The Antigravity SDK exposes search_web for this purpose.
gemini_strategy: We map WebSearch directly to the SDK's search_web tool, maintaining formatting guidelines for markdown hyperlinks and citations.

name: Tool Parameter: Computer action
description: Action parameter options for the Chrome browser computer tool
audit_details: Defines the action parameter options (clicks, type, scroll, key presses) for the browser-based computer automation tool.
gemini_rationale: Computer use parameters are mapped to the Chrome DevTools MCP action options.
gemini_strategy: We map these parameters to our Chrome DevTools MCP tools, ensuring actions like click and type are verified via screenshots.
